titleStmt.title: SII 33.8: original edition by K. Munirathnam and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 8. (A.R. No. 114 of 1946.) Alavalapāḍu, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.

summary.p: Slab set up at the entrance of the Chennakēśava temple.
This is dated Śaka 1705, Śōbhakṛit, Vaiśākha śu. 10. corresponding to 1782 A.D. April 23, Tuesday.

Records that Timmakka, wife of Narasāreḍḍi, son of Yidāreḍḍi of Gaukanapalle and daughter of Koṇḍāreḍḍi of Alavalapāḍu, renovated the temples of the gods Chennakēśava and Sāṁba (Śiva) and restored the daily offerings.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu
svastiśrī vijayābhyuda
ya śālivāhana śakava¨rṣa(ṁ)
bbulu 1705 Agunanēṭi śō
bhakṛtu nāmasaṁvatsara (vai)śākha
śu 10 lu Aluvalapāṭi yu
ra koṁḍāreḍi kumā¨rte gāka
na palle yidāreḍi kumāruḍu
narasāreḍi peṁḍlāmu tiṁmakkagāru
śrī ceṁnnakēśavasvāmi sāṁba
ni dēvaḷālu khilapaḍi vuṁḍeganuka
yī dinaṁ dēvaḷālu kaṭṭiṁci saṁ
prōkṣaṇa cēyiṁci nityōtsavamu
lu cēyiṁccanu
Digital edition of SII 33.8 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
